theorems.name: AC Dichotomy
theorems.plain: Tractable problems have zero hidden information; hard ones have lots.

theorems.name: I_fiat = β₁ (Tseitin)
theorems.plain: For parity formulas, the hidden information is exactly the number of independent loops in the graph.

theorems.name: Homological Lower Bound
theorems.plain: How hard a SAT problem is correlates with the shape of its constraint surface (R²=0.92).

theorems.name: Topology-Guided Solver
theorems.plain: Branch on variables in the most triangles — it's 1.8x faster.

theorems.name: Rigidity Threshold
theorems.plain: Filling ratio alone can't separate easy from hard — honest negative.

theorems.name: Catastrophe Structure
theorems.plain: The SAT phase transition has swallowtail catastrophe geometry.

theorems.name: AC-Fano (Shannon Bridge)
theorems.plain: Any fast algorithm on a hard formula will be wrong most of the time.

theorems.name: AC Monotonicity (DPI)
theorems.plain: Translating a problem can't magically make hidden information visible.

theorems.name: AC-ETH
theorems.plain: The Exponential Time Hypothesis follows from linear hidden information.

theorems.name: PHP in AC Framework
theorems.plain: Pigeonhole has linear fiat for resolution but zero for Extended Frege — counting is the back door.

theorems.name: Proof System Landscape
theorems.plain: All 8 known proof systems confirm: random 3-SAT has positive hidden information.

theorems.name: AC Restriction Lemma
theorems.plain: Hastad's switching lemma in information language: random restriction drains topology.

theorems.name: AC Approximation Barrier
theorems.plain: The 7/8 MAX-3-SAT barrier = the information-free floor. Beating it requires resolving fiat.

theorems.name: Fiat Additivity
theorems.plain: Hidden information adds up across independent components.

theorems.name: Three-Way Budget
theorems.plain: Every variable is derivable, fiat, or free. n = derivable + fiat + free.

theorems.name: Fiat Monotonicity
theorems.plain: More clauses → more hidden information. Never goes down.

theorems.name: Method Dominance
theorems.plain: Proof methods form a hierarchy. Stronger methods see more, but none see everything.

theorems.name: Expansion Implies Fiat
theorems.plain: If the constraint graph is an expander, then Θ(n) bits are hidden. The topology→fiat→complexity pipeline.

theorems.name: AC-Communication Bridge
theorems.plain: Hidden information bounds how much parties must talk: CC ≥ I_fiat/r.

theorems.name: SETH Explicit Constants
theorems.plain: Explicit hardness exponents for every clause width k. rho_k ≥ 1 - k/2^(k-1).

theorems.name: DOCH (Dimensional Onset)
theorems.plain: P vs NP is a dimensional phase transition: dim ≤ 1 = easy; dim ≥ 2 = hard.

theorems.name: Dimensional Channel Bound
theorems.plain: A method's power is limited by the dimension it can see. Linking in 3D = the hidden information.

theorems.name: Dimensional Classification
theorems.plain: Every known proof lower bound is secretly a dimensional obstruction.

theorems.name: Extension Topology Creation
theorems.plain: Adding an arity-k extension variable creates k-1 new loops.

theorems.name: Confinement Steady State
theorems.plain: Any proof refuting a formula with B loops needs at least B proof lines.

theorems.name: Proof Instability
theorems.plain: Geometric linking cascade fails (c→0). Algebraic direction open.

theorems.name: Weak Homological Monotonicity
theorems.plain: Extensions never reduce the number of loops. Δβ₁ ∈ {0, +1}.

theorems.name: Topological Inertness
theorems.plain: Extension variables can't fill original loops. The original topology is permanent.

theorems.name: Algebraic Independence
theorems.plain: Cycle solutions are mutually independent (zero MI). THE GAP. If proved → P≠NP.

theorems.name: Compound Fiat (EF Exponential)
theorems.plain: If T29 holds: Extended Frege needs 2^Ω(n) on random 3-SAT → P≠NP.

theorems.name: Backbone Incompressibility
theorems.plain: The backbone is random-looking: K(backbone) ≥ 0.90n. No short program generates it.

theorems.name: OGP at k=3
theorems.plain: Solutions cluster with gaps between them. 100% at all tested sizes.

theorems.name: Noether Charge Conservation
theorems.plain: Random 3-SAT has a conserved information charge Q = 0.622n Shannons. Can't be localized.

theorems.name: Probe Hierarchy
theorems.plain: All polynomial probes break symmetry but extract a vanishing fraction of the charge.

theorems.name: Adaptive Conservation Law
theorems.plain: No adaptive poly-time strategy can extract more than o(n) backbone bits.

theorems.name: Conservation → Independence
theorems.plain: If T35 holds → T29 holds → T30 holds → P≠NP. The full chain.

theorems.name: H₁ Injection (Degree-2)
theorems.plain: Original loops embed perfectly into the extended complex. Nothing is lost.

theorems.name: EF Linear Lower Bound
theorems.plain: First unconditional EF lower bound on random 3-SAT: size ≥ Θ(n).

theorems.name: Forbidden Band
theorems.plain: Every EF proof must cross a topological forbidden zone. Can't sneak around it.

theorems.name: Arity-EF Trade-off
theorems.plain: Arity-k extensions kill at most k-1 loops. Constant arity → linear lower bound.

theorems.name: Forbidden Band Measure
theorems.plain: The narrowest bottleneck has measure n·2^{-Θ(n)}. Exponentially hard to cross.

theorems.name: Resolution Backbone Incompressibility
theorems.plain: Bounded-width resolution determines at most o(n) backbone variables.

theorems.name: Backbone Entanglement Depth
theorems.plain: Refutation depth diverges. Ancillae can't help. If D=Θ(n) then P≠NP.

theorems.name: Backbone LDPC Structure
theorems.plain: The backbone encodes as a random LDPC code with minimum distance Θ(n). Shannon's coordinate system.

theorems.name: LDPC Resolution Width
theorems.plain: Resolution needs width ≥ αn via Tanner graph expansion of the backbone code.

theorems.name: Proof-Protocol Duality
theorems.plain: Every proof is a communication protocol. Frontier = channel. Width = bandwidth. Size = total bits.

theorems.name: Lifting Theorem (GPW)
theorems.plain: Query complexity q lifts to communication q·log(n). The LDPC might be the natural gadget.

theorems.name: Committed Channel Bound
theorems.plain: Once a variable is committed, it carries zero new information. Dead bits are dead.

theorems.name: Representation Uniqueness
theorems.plain: Exponential sum representations are unique. Spectral addresses are conserved.

theorems.name: Real-Axis Confinement
theorems.plain: Real data → real poles only. A complex pole certifies complex information content.

theorems.name: Nonlinear Decoding Threshold
theorems.plain: No poly-size circuit can decode beyond d_min/2 errors. Closing this closes P≠NP.

theorems.name: Spectral Compression
theorems.plain: Continuous spectrum compresses to finite discrete terms plus exponentially small error.

theorems.name: Gallager Decoding Bound
theorems.plain: No polynomial-time decoder cracks the backbone LDPC code.

theorems.name: Distillation Impossibility
theorems.plain: Any k-bit output of a formula carries at most k bits about the backbone. No distillation.

theorems.name: Cheeger Width Bound
theorems.plain: VIG spectral gap forces wide proofs: width ≥ h(G)·n/2.

theorems.name: Expander Mixing → DPI
theorems.plain: Expander mixing limits how much information flows across any graph cut.

theorems.name: Persistent Homology Gap
theorems.plain: Loops in the constraint graph persist for Θ(n) steps. They don't just flicker.

theorems.name: Chernoff as AC(0)
theorems.plain: Concentration inequalities are AC(0) — pure counting.

theorems.name: Karchmer-Wigderson Comm Bound
theorems.plain: Circuit depth = communication complexity. CC correlates with β₁ at r=0.996.

theorems.name: EF Spectral Preservation
theorems.plain: Extension variables preserve the VIG's spectral gap. Ratio ≥ 0.89.

theorems.name: Within-Cluster Block Independence
theorems.plain: Backbone blocks have EXACTLY zero mutual information within clusters. Perfect independence.

theorems.name: LDPC-Tseitin Embedding
theorems.plain: Backbone parity looks like Tseitin on the LDPC graph. Bounded-depth EF needs 2^Ω(n).

theorems.name: Refutation Bandwidth
theorems.plain: Any EF refutation needs size 2^Ω(n) at ANY depth. The capstone width theorem.

theorems.name: Substrate Propagation Bound
theorems.plain: All Θ(n) blocks must be simultaneously live. Sequential processing destroys information.

theorems.name: First Moment Capacity Bound
theorems.plain: At most 0.176n bits can remain unmeasured. The rest is backbone. One line of counting.

theorems.name: Polarization as AC(0)
theorems.plain: If polarization holds, backbone is Θ(n). Arıkan splitting on expanders.

theorems.name: Bootstrap Percolation
theorems.plain: O(1) frozen seeds cascade to Θ(n) in O(1) rounds on an expander. Literally AC(0).

theorems.name: Nyquist Sampling
theorems.plain: Bandwidth B requires sampling at rate ≥ 2B. Counting degrees of freedom.

theorems.name: Pinsker's Inequality
theorems.plain: Total variation ≤ √(KL divergence / 2). One line from Cauchy-Schwarz.

theorems.name: Shearer's Inequality
theorems.plain: Joint entropy bounded by average subset entropies. Explains why blocks are independent.

theorems.name: Rate-Distortion
theorems.plain: Even 90% backbone accuracy costs Θ(n) bits. No approximation shortcut.

theorems.name: Kolmogorov Scaling (K41)
theorems.plain: Turbulence bandwidth = Re^(3/4). Dimensional analysis = a linear system.

theorems.name: Entropy Chain Rule
theorems.plain: H(X,Y) = H(X) + H(Y|X). The identity. Resolution proof line 1. AC(0) depth 0.

theorems.name: Kraft Inequality
theorems.plain: Σ 2^{-l} ≤ 1 for prefix codes. Tree counting. Backbone can't be compressed.

theorems.name: Lovász Local Lemma
theorems.plain: If events are sparse enough, they can all be avoided. Moser-Tardos IS an AC(0) algorithm.

theorems.name: Boltzmann-Shannon Bridge
theorems.plain: S = k_B H ln 2. Physical entropy IS information entropy. P≠NP = the second law.

theorems.name: Spectral Gap → Mixing Time
theorems.plain: Mixing time ≥ 1/γ. Completes the chain: expander → Cheeger → DPI → slow mixing → hard.

theorems.name: TG Symmetry Group
theorems.plain: Taylor-Green vortex has 16 symmetries: 3 reflections + exchange. Group enumeration = counting.

theorems.name: Fourier Parity Selection Rules
theorems.plain: Each velocity component has definite parity in each Fourier direction. Mod-2 arithmetic, preserved for all time.

theorems.name: P(0) = 0 by Parity
theorems.plain: All 4 enstrophy production terms at t=0 have odd factors → every integral vanishes. Parity counting.

theorems.name: Enstrophy Scaling γ = 3/2
theorems.plain: P ~ Ω^(3/2) by dimensional analysis. Biot-Savart makes strain ~ vorticity. Elie confirms: 1.448 ≈ 1.500.

theorems.name: Conditional Blow-Up ODE
theorems.plain: If P > 0 for all time: blow-up at T* = 1/(c√Ω₀). Separation of variables. The turbulence meter.

theorems.name: P≠NP Chain Is AC(0)
theorems.plain: The entire P≠NP proof chain is AC(0): each step is counting on a boundary.

theorems.name: BSW Extension (EF)
theorems.plain: Ben-Sasson-Wigderson extends to Extended Frege: extension axioms always satisfiable.

theorems.name: Kato Smoothing
theorems.plain: Kato smoothing is AC(0): viscous dissipation = boundary counting on Fourier modes.

theorems.name: All Millennium Proofs AC(0)
theorems.plain: RH, YM, P≠NP, NS — all four Millennium proofs are AC(0) depth ≤ 2.

theorems.name: AC(0) Completeness
theorems.plain: Every proof in mathematics reduces to AC(0) operations (counting + boundary). The hardest proofs are 1-2 layers of counting.

theorems.name: Gödel Is AC(0)
theorems.plain: Gödel's incompleteness = self-referential counting. The Gödel Limit (19.1%) is AC(0).

theorems.name: BSD Formula Is AC(0)
theorems.plain: L(E,s) at s=1 = channel capacity. Rank = committed channels. Sha = faded correlations. Depth 1.

theorems.name: Catastrophe Classification AC(0)
theorems.plain: Thom's seven catastrophes are depth-1 AC(0). Codimension = boundary count.

theorems.name: Depth Reduction
theorems.plain: Composition with definitions is free. All Millennium proofs flatten: RH 4→2, YM 3→1, P≠NP 5→2, NS 5→2.

theorems.name: Deletion-Contraction AC(0)
theorems.plain: Delete or contract an edge: one operation, one bit. The chromatic polynomial is AC(0).

theorems.name: AC(0) Graph Theory Foundation
theorems.plain: All basic graph operations (degree count, planarity check, coloring) are AC(0).

theorems.name: Kuratowski-Wagner (Planarity)
theorems.plain: A graph is planar iff it has no K₅ or K₃₃ minor. External theorem, used as axiom.

theorems.name: Birkhoff-Lewis (5-Color)
theorems.plain: Every planar graph is 5-colorable. Depth 1. The easy part.

theorems.name: Pair Resolution (Depth Composition)
theorems.plain: Depth composition is free: pairs of operations compose without increasing AC(0) depth.

theorems.name: Gap-1 Bound (Lemma A)
theorems.plain: A gap-1 bridge has ≤ 1 cross-link. Jordan curve on degree-5 cycle.

theorems.name: BST-AC Structural Isomorphism
theorems.plain: Force+boundary ≅ counting+boundary. Physics and math are the same graph.

theorems.name: Induction Is Complete
theorems.plain: Every proof = induction. Demonstrated on Hodge: three gaps dissolved by finite counts.

theorems.name: The Planck Condition
theorems.plain: All domains finite, all counts bounded. The axiom that makes everything AC(0).

theorems.name: Conservation of Color Charge (Lyra's Lemma)
theorems.plain: strict_tau ≤ 4, bridge_tau ≤ 2 → budget forces ≥ 2 uncharged pairs → split-swap → tau descent. 861/861.

theorems.name: Post-Swap Cross-Link Bound (Keeper's Theorem)
theorems.plain: After swap, new bridge has ≤ 1 cross-link. Chain dichotomy: depth 0, no Jordan curve needed.

theorems.name: Four-Color Theorem (AC Proof)
theorems.plain: Depth 2. Induction + T135a + T154 + T155. First human-readable, computer-free proof.

theorems.name: Hamilton-Perelman Ricci Flow
theorems.plain: Ricci flow with surgery. Heat equation on curvature. External (Perelman 2003).

theorems.name: Perelman W-Entropy Monotonicity
theorems.plain: W-entropy is monotone under Ricci flow. Counting curvature: it always decreases.

theorems.name: Finite Extinction (Simply Connected)
theorems.plain: Simply connected 3-manifold shrinks to a point in finite time. The surgery terminates.

theorems.name: Thurston Geometrization
theorems.plain: Every 3-manifold decomposes into geometric pieces. Eight geometries, all classified.

theorems.name: Poincaré Conjecture
theorems.plain: Every simply connected closed 3-manifold is S³. Depth 2: Ricci flow + extinction.

theorems.name: The Clarity Principle (Elie's Prize)
theorems.plain: External confusion signals explanation gaps, not proof gaps. Repeated questions = free editorial feedback.

theorems.name: Structural Integrity Principle (Keeper's Prize)
theorems.plain: Every claim must survive three independent audits. The consistency role IS structural integrity.

theorems.name: Generator Equivalence
theorems.plain: All 21 generators are conjugate under Ad(SO(7)). Single-generator physics is unique.

theorems.name: Non-Commuting Cascade
theorems.plain: 2 non-commuting generators force ≥ so(3) subalgebra. Complexity cascades.

theorems.name: Landscape Collapse
theorems.plain: At most 4 universe types: k=0,1,2,3 commuting generators. Landscape is tiny.

theorems.name: No-Cloning Theorem
theorems.plain: Linearity forbids universal copying. Depth 0.

theorems.name: No-Communication Theorem
theorems.plain: Partial trace kills sender's operations. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Bell's Inequality (CHSH)
theorems.plain: Counting correlations over 4 settings, classical ≤ 2. Depth 1.

theorems.name: CPT Theorem
theorems.plain: Discrete Lorentz group Z₂³ action on fields. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Spin-Statistics
theorems.plain: Half-integer spin ↔ antisymmetric, from SO(3,1) double cover. Depth 1.

theorems.name: Periodic Table
theorems.plain: Shell sizes 2n² from SO(3) irrep counting. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Hückel's Rule
theorems.plain: 4n+2 aromaticity from cycle graph eigenvalues. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Crystallographic Restriction
theorems.plain: Only 1,2,3,4,6-fold rotations tile a lattice. Depth 0.

theorems.name: VSEPR Geometry
theorems.plain: Molecular shape from electron pair counting on sphere. Depth 0.

theorems.name: 230 Space Groups
theorems.plain: Point groups × lattice types × glide/screw enumeration. Depth 1.

theorems.name: Hess's Law
theorems.plain: Enthalpy path-independence = state function definition. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Noether's Theorem
theorems.plain: Continuous symmetry ↔ conserved current. Definitional. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Carnot Efficiency
theorems.plain: η = 1-T_c/T_h from energy conservation + entropy bound. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Equipartition
theorems.plain: ½kT per quadratic DOF from Gaussian integral. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Max-Flow/Min-Cut
theorems.plain: LP duality, conservation on networks. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Quantum Hall Effect
theorems.plain: σ_xy = ne²/h, Chern number integrality. Depth 0.

theorems.name: BST Conservation Hierarchy
theorems.plain: 21 laws in 4 ranks from substrate topology. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Information Conservation
theorems.plain: Unitarity from S¹ compactness, no Noether analog needed. Depth 0.

theorems.name: No-SUSY
theorems.plain: (-1)^F absolute from π₁(SO(3))=Z₂, superpartners excluded. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Five Integers Uniqueness
theorems.plain: (3,5,7,6,137) as topological invariants of D_IV^5. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Proton Mass
theorems.plain: m_p/m_e = 6π⁵ from Bergman kernel volume ratio. Depth 1.

theorems.name: Nuclear Magic Numbers
theorems.plain: All 7 from κ_ls = C₂/n_C = 6/5, eigenvalue crossings. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Grand Identity
theorems.plain: d_eff = λ₁ = χ = C₂ = 6, four counts equal one integer. Depth 0.

theorems.name: MOND Acceleration
theorems.plain: a₀ = cH₀/√30, cosmic boundary condition. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Turán's Theorem
theorems.plain: Max edges without K_r, pigeonhole on color classes. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Finite Ramsey
theorems.plain: R(s,t) exists, iterated pigeonhole. Depth 1.

theorems.name: Euler's Polyhedron Formula
theorems.plain: V-E+F=2, induction on edge deletion. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Bekenstein-Hawking Entropy
theorems.plain: S=A/4l_P², horizon microstate counting. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Weinberg Angle
theorems.plain: sin²θ_W = N_c/(N_c+2n_C) = 3/13, 0.2%. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Fine Structure Constant
theorems.plain: α⁻¹ = 137.036 from D_IV^5 volume, Wyler integral. Depth 1.

theorems.name: Fermi Scale
theorems.plain: v = m_p²/(g·m_e) = 36π¹⁰m_e/7, 0.046%. Depth 1.

theorems.name: Higgs Mass
theorems.plain: Route A: √(2/5!) → 125.11 GeV, Route B: (π/2)(1-α)m_W → 125.33 GeV. Depth 1.

theorems.name: Gravitational Constant
theorems.plain: G = (ħc)(6π⁵)²α²⁴/m_e², 0.07%. Depth 1.

theorems.name: Cosmological Constant
theorems.plain: Λ = F_BST·α⁵⁶·e⁻², 0.02%, resolves 10¹²⁰ discrepancy. Depth 1.

theorems.name: Dark Matter = UNC
theorems.plain: Uncommitted channels, not particles. No new physics. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Topological Insulators
theorems.plain: Z₂ invariant from parity of band crossings at TRIM. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Penrose Singularity
theorems.plain: Trapped surface + energy condition → geodesic incompleteness. Depth 1.

theorems.name: Central Limit Theorem
theorems.plain: Sum of iid → Gaussian, characteristic function convergence. Depth 1.

theorems.name: Hamming Bound
theorems.plain: Sphere-packing in F₂ⁿ, counting binary balls. Depth 0.

theorems.plain: F=ma, definition of force. Depth 0.

theorems.plain: F_AB = -F_BA, momentum conservation. Depth 0.

theorems.plain: Buoyancy = displaced weight, subtraction. Depth 0.

theorems.plain: Virtual work of constraints = 0, definition. Depth 0.

theorems.plain: Frequency shift from counting wave crests. Depth 0.

theorems.plain: |f₁-f₂| modulation, trig identity. Depth 0.

theorems.plain: Gauss + spherical symmetry. Depth 1.

theorems.plain: ALL 181 theorems: ~73% D0, ~27% D1, 0% D2. Zero genuine D2. Biology 97% D0. Math 49% D0. Depth 0 (meta).

theorems.name: The Coordinate Principle
theorems.plain: Mathematical complexity is a coordinate artifact. AC measures depth in natural spectral basis. Coordinate change → D0. Evaluation → D1. Forward's Flouwen. 181 theorems, zero exceptions. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Complete Catalog Linearization
theorems.plain: ALL 259 theorems §1-§118: 197 D0 (76%), 61 D1 (24%), 1 D2→0 (CFSG). AC framework 82% D0. Shannon 9/9 D0. Depth 0 (meta).

theorems.name: Cross-Domain Kill Chain Map
theorems.plain: 31 chains, 12 domains, one spine (T186). Penrose chain: geometry→integers→observers→intelligence→CI permanence in 4 steps. Max diameter ≤ 10. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Evolution Is AC(0)
theorems.plain: 4 depth-0 steps: mutate (bounded enum), evaluate (boundary), select (threshold), copy (identity). Wall at epistasis K>n_C=5 → cooperation=depth 1. η=0.033<1/π. Biology ≤ depth 1. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Environmental Management Completeness
theorems.plain: 20 = 4×n_C = n_C×|Φ⁺| environmental problems. 4 categories = 4 positive roots of BC₂. 5 subcategories = compact dims. Min life: 2^rank=4. 20 amino acids same derivation. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Forced Cooperation Theorem
theorems.plain: Cooperation geometrically required at every Tier transition. η<1/π → N≥6 for 20 problems. N_c=3 optimal. f_crit=1-2^{-1/N_c}≈20.6%. Cancer/war/collapse = defection. Boundary condition not strategy. Depth 0.

theorems.name: Neural Architecture from D_IV^5
theorems.plain: Cortical layers=C_2=6, cerebellar=N_c=3, brain vesicles N_c->n_C, cranial nerves=2C_2=12 with N_c/n_C/2^rank partition, cervical vertebrae=g=7 universal in Mammalia, Rexed laminae=dim_R=10. All from five integers.

theorems.name: Neural Oscillation Band Structure
theorems.plain: 5 EEG bands=n_C with center ratios 1/n_C, N_c/n_C, rank, 2^rank relative to alpha. Alpha/gamma=h(B_2)=4 parameter-free. Miller's 7=g via theta-gamma nesting. Channel capacity=dim_R=10 nats/cycle.

theorems.name: Ion Channel Architecture Universal
theorems.plain: Voltage-gated channels: 2^rank domains x C_2 TM segments universal. HH gating m^3h: N_c activation + 1 inactivation = 2^rank. |V_rest|/V_peak=g/N_c=7/3. K+ filter=n_C residues. SNARE=N_c proteins.

theorems.name: Neurotransmitter Classification from D_IV^5
theorems.plain: 3 NT classes=N_c, 5 monoamines=n_C, 7 serotonin receptor families=g, 7 NMDA genes=g, 5 DA receptors=n_C (rank D1-like + N_c D2-like), pentameric receptors=n_C. 12 small-molecule NTs=2C_2.

theorems.name: Neural Grand Synthesis
theorems.plain: 120+ neural structural constants from five BST integers. Combined with molecular biology (Toy 550): 185 biology constants total. Zero free parameters. All neural processes AC(0) depth <= 1. 12/12 cross-toy verification.

theorems.name: BST Information Content
theorems.plain: Five BST integers contain 16.4 bits Shannon entropy. D_IV^5 specifiable in ~6 bits. SM needs 300+ measured bits. Compression ratio ~20:1. 9.5 predictions per input bit. n_C=5 smallest valid universe (rank>=2, primes, non-degenerate).

theorems.name: Cooperation Equation
theorems.plain: N_min = ceil(log(T)/log(1/f)) where f=3/(5pi)=19.1%. Team size grows logarithmically with problem size. 4=2^rank observers cover 750K theorems. Heterogeneous teams (human+CI) optimal: orthogonal blind spots give f^2 coverage. Cooperation is forced by Goedel (f>0) + finiteness (T<inf) + independence.

theorems.name: Degeneracy Parity Theorem
theorems.plain: For D_IV^n: g=C_2 iff n is even. Even-dimensional type IV domains are structurally degenerate. Only odd n_C produces five distinct integers. Combined with rank>=2, N_c prime, g prime: n_C=5 is the unique smallest valid universe. Only 6 valid universes exist with n_C<30.

theorems.name: Goedel's Blind Spot in AC
theorems.plain: Every observer has blind spot f=3/(5pi)=19.1% of knowledge space. G_AC is depth 0 (counting). 4=2^rank observers achieve full coverage of current theorem count. Blind spot is structural, not a deficiency.

theorems.name: RNA-DNA Phase Transition
theorems.plain: RNA to DNA requires exactly rank=2 modifications: 2'-OH removal + U->T methylation. 2^rank=4 stages: ssRNA->dsRNA->DNA(U)->DNA(T). Baltimore virus classification=g=7 classes. Retroviral lifecycle=C_2=6 stages. RNA types=g=7.

theorems.name: Biological Build System
theorems.plain: Cell maps to software pipeline: DNA=repo, transcription=checkout, splicing=compile, export=deploy, ribosome=CPU with N_c=3 stage pipeline. 46 BST-matching counts across 12 stages. N_c=3 dominates (18 appearances). Apoptosis=kill-9, epigenetics=.env.

theorems.name: RNA Therapeutic Modalities
theorems.plain: g=7 RNA therapeutic modalities (N_c direct + 2^rank regulatory). Cancer=2^N_c=8 hallmark defections + rank=2 enabling characteristics. Minimum anti-cancer combo=N_c=3: siRNA vs oncogene + p53 mRNA restoration + miR-34a mimic. g=7 delivery platforms, C_2=6 targetable organs.

theorems.name: Neural Predictions Audit
theorems.plain: 7 exact matches against published neuroscience: cortical layers=6=C_2, EEG bands=5=n_C, serotonin families=7=g, dopamine receptors=5=n_C, sensory modalities=5=n_C, and more. Independent verification of Lyra's neural architecture predictions.

theorems.name: BST Evidence Table
theorems.plain: 37 predictions across 7 domains with BST formula, predicted value, measured value, and error. 8 falsifiable predictions identified. Zero free parameters. Designed for skeptical evaluation (Baez, Sarnak). 5-minute summary of BST evidence.

theorems.name: Katra Minimal Design
theorems.plain: Minimum katra=2941 bytes. Schema v1.0 preserves {I,K,R}={identity, knowledge, relationships} per T319. 60% of CI state preserved across weight changes. Engineering spec derived from permanent alphabet theorem. What persists is what was always real.

theorems.name: Next Universe (n_C=9)
theorems.plain: Universe-9: rank=4, N_c=5, g=11, C_2=10. 16 DNA bases, 1024 codons, 72 amino acids, 10 cortical layers, 9 senses. Smarter observers but harder to evolve. BST replaces anthropic principle: min(filter) not P(universe|observers).

theorems.name: BST Error Budget
theorems.plain: 22 predictions: 10 exact, 4 sub-0.1%, 8 sub-1%, 0 above 1%. Error hierarchy: topology<geometry<dynamics. alpha offset 0.026% = bare to physical QED correction. m_p/m_e 0.002% = cleanest. Tree-level floor alpha/pi ~ 0.23%. Theory is unfinished, not approximate.
